title: Range-resolution helper for adversarial-project
goal: Add a pure helper that resolves --from-phase N / --to-phase M into {fromSha, toSha} from stored autoCommit.preSha via loadPhase (fromSha = preSha of N; toSha = preSha of M+1, else HEAD when M is last), with plain git-ref passthrough for --from/--to, and hard-fails on missing preSha, invalid ref, empty range, or mixing phase+ref — unit-tested on every failure path. No git-subject derivation.

steps.title: Module + resolveRange signature
steps.detail: New src/range.ts exporting resolveRange(cwd, input) where input = { fromPhase?, toPhase?, from?, to? } and result is the union { ok: true, fromSha, toSha } | { ok: false, error }. No throw. Stub returns a not-implemented error so it typechecks.

steps.title: Reject mixed phase+ref and incomplete bounds
steps.detail: Pure input validation before any git/loadPhase: error if a phase flag is combined with a ref flag in one run; error if a side is missing (need both a from-bound and a to-bound). Covered by unit tests.

steps.title: Resolve phase bounds via loadPhase + preSha
steps.detail: fromSha = preSha of phase N; toSha = preSha of phase M+1 via loadPhase, else headSha when M+1 is absent (M is last). Hard-fail when phase N (or an existing M+1) has no autoCommit.preSha, or phase N not found.

steps.title: Resolve git-ref bounds via rev-parse
steps.detail: Passthrough --from/--to as plain refs; validate each with git rev-parse --verify <ref>^{commit} via runGit; error on invalid/unknown ref; return the resolved SHAs.

steps.title: Reject empty range
steps.detail: After both bounds resolve, error when fromSha === toSha (empty diff range), regardless of phase vs ref mode.

steps.title: Unit tests: every failure path + happy paths
steps.detail: New src/range.test.ts covering happy phase-range, M-is-last to HEAD, ref passthrough, and each failure: mixed phase+ref, missing bound, phase-not-found, missing preSha, invalid ref, empty range. Full test suite + typecheck green.
